danger_signals.meaning: World under existential threat; civilization collapsed or collapsing

danger_signals.visual_form: Laser fire patterns; boss attack animations

danger_signals.meaning: Instant-death environmental hazard; instant life loss

danger_signals.visual_form: Large-scale alien entities; dramatic visual presentation shifts between run-and-gun and shoot-em-up phases
danger_signals.meaning: Critical danger point; escalated threat level

safety_signals.visual_form: Colored floating orbs distributed throughout levels
safety_signals.meaning: Power increase; improved combat capability; player empowerment

safety_signals.visual_form: Player can select 3-7 lives at start; 5-hit tolerance per life
safety_signals.meaning: Margin for error; survivability buffer against mistakes

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Post-apocalyptic human strongholds (implied through setting); Earth defense positions

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Extraterrestrial entity designs (visually distinct from human)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Boss-tier aliens with distinct phase-based attack patterns (run-and-gun vs. shoot-em-up formats)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Devastated Earth landscape; alien corruption of human spaces

world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Referenced through 'remaining inhabitants' language; world already transformed

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Post-apocalyptic environment aesthetic; pre-rendered 3D graphics emphasizing destruction and desolation
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player role is defensive; tasked with protecting remaining survivors; run-and-gun combat format reflects survival-under-siege scenario

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Dynamic shift in visual presentation between standard run-and-gun levels and dedicated shoot-em-up boss phases
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player enters high-intensity combat mode; weapon selection becomes critical; stage hazards (pits) remain lethal

environmental_storytelling.example: Devastated Earth setting communicates world-state of civilization under siege; visual decay tells story of alien impact without dialogue
Player framed as 'special forces soldier tasked with defending Earth and remaining inhabitants' — role establishes faction identity and stakes
Different colored floating orbs signal different laser gun types — visual language for player progression and tactical options without UI explanation
Run-and-gun levels transition to shoot-em-up boss fights — visual/mechanical language communicates enemy escalation and adaptation

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Run-and-gun traversal; horizontal movement with weapon fire
Shift to vertical or arena-based shoot-em-up combat; different input/positioning demands
Projectile attacks; patterns indicating hostility and threat
Multi-phase attack patterns; escalated projectile density; visual transformation between run-and-gun and shoot-em-up phases
Automatic life loss on contact — instant failure state regardless of hit tolerance

notes: Rendering Ranger: R2 uses pre-rendered 3D graphics (a significant technical choice post-Donkey Kong Country success) to communicate post-apocalyptic world-state visually. The game's dual gameplay phases (run-and-gun + shoot-em-up boss encounters) serve as manifestation layer for threat escalation. Faction identity is communicated through role assignment (human special forces vs. alien invaders) rather than explicit color/sigil systems. The T3 data tier reflects reliance on Wikipedia gameplay summary; no primary design documentation available for deeper manifestation analysis. Weapon orb system and life count options create granular danger/safety visual language through object pickup and UI design.
